title: Society for Psychical Research Founded
description: A group of Cambridge scholars, philosophers, and scientists establish the Society for Psychical Research (SPR) in London — the first organisation to apply systematic empirical methods to the investigation of telepathy, clairvoyance, mediumship, and apparitions. Founding members include Henry Sidgwick, Frederic Myers, and Edmund Gurney.
sources: Proceedings of the Society for Psychical Research, Vol. 1 (1882)
Alan Gauld, The Founders of Psychical Research (1968)

title: J.B. Rhine Establishes ESP Laboratory at Duke University
description: Joseph Banks Rhine, working under psychologist William McDougall at Duke University, begins systematic forced-choice experiments using Zener cards to test for extrasensory perception. Rhine's programme establishes the first academic parapsychology laboratory and introduces the term 'ESP' into scientific discourse.
sources: J.B. Rhine, Extra-Sensory Perception (1934)
Rhine Research Center archives

title: Foo Fighters Reported by Allied Aircrews
description: Allied bomber and fighter crews operating over Europe and the Pacific begin reporting anomalous luminous objects — small, fast-moving spheres of light — that manoeuvre alongside aircraft without attacking. German and Japanese pilots make equivalent reports, ruling out a single-nation experimental programme. The objects become known as 'Foo Fighters'. No conventional explanation has been established for all reported cases.
sources: Randle, K. and Estes, R., Faces of the Visitors (1997)
Vesco, R. and Childress, D., Man-Made UFOs 1944–1994 (1994)
Contemporary USAAF mission reports, National Archives

title: Kenneth Arnold Sighting Near Mount Rainier
description: Civilian pilot Kenneth Arnold reports observing nine crescent-shaped objects flying in formation at high speed near Mount Rainier, Washington. Arnold describes their movement as resembling 'a saucer skipping across water,' giving rise to the term 'flying saucer.' The report receives wide national press attention and triggers a wave of subsequent sightings across the United States.
sources: Arnold, K., Report to Army Air Corps intelligence officer, Pendleton, Oregon, June 25, 1947
Arnold, K. and Palmer, R., The Coming of the Saucers (1952)
Project Blue Book Historical Record Card, Case No. 33

title: Roswell Army Air Field Press Release
description: Colonel William Blanchard's public information officer issues a press release announcing the recovery of a 'flying disc' from a ranch near Roswell, New Mexico. The statement is retracted within hours by General Ramey at Fort Worth.
sources: Roswell Daily Record, July 8, 1947
Associated Press wire, July 8, 1947
USAF Report on Roswell, 1994

title: Project Sign Established at Wright-Patterson AFB
description: The U.S. Air Force formally establishes Project Sign, the first official programme for investigating unidentified flying objects, under the Air Materiel Command at Wright-Patterson Air Force Base, Ohio. The programme is tasked with collecting, collating, evaluating, and distributing all information concerning UAP sightings, with priority on assessing any national security threat.
sources: Air Materiel Command letter, December 30, 1947 (declassified)
Ruppelt, E.J., The Report on Unidentified Flying Objects (1956)

title: Project Blue Book Established
description: The U.S. Air Force establishes Project Blue Book as its primary ongoing UFO investigation unit, replacing Project Grudge. Captain Edward Ruppelt is named director. Over 17 years, Blue Book investigates 12,618 reported sightings, of which 701 remain 'unidentified' at the programme's closure. J. Allen Hynek serves as scientific consultant throughout.
sources: Ruppelt, E.J., The Report on Unidentified Flying Objects (1956)
Project Blue Book Archive, National Archives

title: Washington D.C. Radar Incursions
description: Multiple unidentified radar returns tracked over restricted airspace above Washington D.C. by Andrews AFB and Washington National Airport. Visual confirmation reported by pilots and ground crew over two consecutive weekends.
sources: Project Blue Book files, Case 1501
Washington Post, July 21, 1952

title: Socorro, New Mexico — Lonnie Zamora Sighting
description: Police officer Lonnie Zamora reports observing an egg-shaped craft on the ground attended by two small humanoid figures outside Socorro, New Mexico. Physical trace evidence including scorched vegetation and indentations was documented by investigators.
sources: Project Blue Book investigation report
Coral and Jim Lorenzen, UFOs: The Whole Story (1969)

title: Project Blue Book Disbanded
description: Following the University of Colorado's Condon Report, which concluded that further UFO investigation was unlikely to yield scientific benefit, the U.S. Air Force officially disbands Project Blue Book on December 17, 1969. The programme's 12,618-case archive is eventually declassified and transferred to the National Archives. J. Allen Hynek publicly disputes the Condon Report's conclusions.
sources: USAF press release, December 17, 1969
Condon, E.U. (ed.), Scientific Study of Unidentified Flying Objects (1969)
Hynek, J.A., The UFO Experience (1972)

title: SRI Remote Viewing Programme Begins
description: Physicists Russell Targ and Harold Puthoff at Stanford Research Institute (SRI) begin government-contracted laboratory investigations of remote viewing and other anomalous cognition phenomena. Initial work involves subjects Ingo Swann and Pat Price. CIA funding is provided under contract with SRI.
sources: Russell Targ and Harold Puthoff, 'Information Transmission Under Conditions of Sensory Shielding,' Nature (1974)
CIA FOIA release, Stargate programme documents

title: Targ and Puthoff Remote Viewing Results Published in Nature
description: A paper by Russell Targ and Harold Puthoff reporting statistically significant results in remote viewing experiments with Ingo Swann and Pat Price is published in Nature, Vol. 251. The paper prompts sustained scientific debate. Results from 51 outdoor target trials showed above-chance correspondence by independent blind judges.
sources: Targ, R. and Puthoff, H.E., Nature 251, 602–607 (1974)

title: Moody Publishes Life After Life
description: Physician Raymond Moody publishes Life After Life, compiling accounts from over 150 individuals resuscitated from clinical death or near-death. The book introduces the term 'near-death experience' into scientific and popular discourse and identifies a recurrent phenomenological pattern including out-of-body perception, tunnel experience, and life review.
sources: Raymond Moody, Life After Life (1975)
International Association for Near-Death Studies

title: Stevenson Publishes Cases of the Reincarnation Type, Volume I
description: Psychiatrist Ian Stevenson of the University of Virginia publishes the first volume of Cases of the Reincarnation Type, presenting systematic field investigations of children reporting memories of previous lives. The four-volume series ultimately documents over 2,500 cases with detailed correspondence analysis.
sources: Ian Stevenson, Cases of the Reincarnation Type Vol. I: India (1975)
University of Virginia Division of Perceptual Studies

title: Tehran F-4 Intercept Encounter
description: Two Iranian Air Force F-4 Phantom fighters scrambled to intercept an unidentified aerial object over Tehran experience sequential avionics and weapons system failures at close range. A DIA report filed the following day characterises the case as meeting all criteria for a valid UAP study, citing radar-visual correlation, credible trained witnesses, electromagnetic effects, and sustained duration.
sources: DIA Report: Iran: Unidentified Flying Object, September 19, 1976 (declassified)
Kean, L., UFOs: Generals, Pilots, and Government Officials Go on the Record (2010)

title: Princeton PEAR Laboratory Founded
description: Robert G. Jahn, Dean of Engineering at Princeton University, establishes the Princeton Engineering Anomalies Research (PEAR) laboratory to conduct systematic studies of possible mind-matter interaction using electronic random event generators. The laboratory operates until 2007 and accumulates approximately 2.5 million experimental trials.
sources: Robert G. Jahn and Brenda J. Dunne, Margins of Reality (1987)
PEAR Technical Note Series, Princeton University

title: Rendlesham Forest Incident
description: USAF security personnel at RAF Woodbridge, Suffolk, England, observe and approach an unidentified structured object in Rendlesham Forest. Over the following two nights, Deputy Base Commander Lieutenant Colonel Charles Halt leads a team that observes a pulsing object directing light beams toward the base. Physical trace evidence, elevated radiation readings, and the official Halt memorandum document the incidents.
sources: Halt, C., Memorandum to UK Ministry of Defence, January 13, 1981 (declassified 1983)
UK National Archives, UFO Files series (2008–2013)

title: Belgium Black Triangle Wave Begins
description: The first of a series of mass sightings of large, silent triangular objects over Belgium, initiating a wave that continues through 1990. At its peak, over 13,000 witnesses report sightings. The Belgian Air Force launches F-16 intercepts after radar confirmation; pilots report radar locks followed by sudden evasive manoeuvres. The Belgian Air Force releases full investigation files and acknowledges the objects were unidentified.
sources: Belgian Air Force investigation, released publicly 1990
Meessen, A., Journal of Scientific Exploration 23(3), 2009
Kean, L., UFOs: Generals, Pilots, and Government Officials Go on the Record (2010), Chapter 1

title: Bem and Honorton Autoganzfeld Meta-Analysis Published
description: Psychologist Daryl Bem and parapsychologist Charles Honorton publish a meta-analysis of 11 autoganzfeld ESP studies in Psychological Bulletin, reporting a cumulative hit rate significantly above the 25% chance baseline (z = 6.60, p < 10⁻¹¹). The publication in a mainstream psychology journal marks a significant moment of crossover into academic visibility.
sources: Bem, D.J. and Honorton, C., Psychological Bulletin 115(1), 4–18 (1994)

title: US Government Stargate Programme Declassified
description: Following a CIA-commissioned review by the American Institutes for Research, the existence of the US government's remote viewing research programme — known under successive code names including GRILL FLAME, CENTER LANE, and STARGATE — is publicly acknowledged. The programme had operated since 1972 at SRI and SAIC, with funding across multiple agencies.
sources: AIR Report: An Evaluation of Remote Viewing, September 1995
Utts, J., Journal of Scientific Exploration 10(1), 3–30 (1996)
Hyman, R., Journal of Scientific Exploration 10(1), 31–58 (1996)

title: Phoenix Lights Mass Sighting
description: Thousands of witnesses across Arizona and Nevada report a massive V-shaped formation of lights moving silently from northwest to southeast over a corridor of several hundred miles. A separate set of stationary lights appears over Phoenix later in the evening. Arizona Governor Fife Symington, who held a mocking press conference at the time, later disclosed that he himself witnessed a large structured craft and that his response had been intended to manage public anxiety.
sources: National UFO Reporting Center, March 1997 database
Symington, F., testimony and interviews, 2007
Kitei, L., The Phoenix Lights (2004)

title: Van Lommel NDE Prospective Study Published in The Lancet
description: Cardiologist Pim van Lommel and colleagues publish a prospective study of 344 cardiac arrest survivors in The Lancet, finding that 18% reported an NDE. The study finds no correlation between NDE occurrence and duration of unconsciousness, medication, or prior awareness of NDEs, and documents cases with apparently accurate out-of-body perceptions during verified cardiac standstill.
sources: van Lommel, P. et al., The Lancet 358(9298), 2039–2045 (2001)

title: USS Nimitz FLIR Encounter
description: F/A-18 pilots from the USS Nimitz carrier strike group intercept an unidentified aerial object approximately 100 miles southwest of San Diego. Commander David Fravor reports a close visual encounter with a white oblong object exhibiting anomalous flight characteristics. Footage officially released by DoD in 2020.
sources: DoD FOIA release, April 2020
New York Times, December 16, 2017
UAPTF Preliminary Assessment, 2021

title: Terminal Lucidity Formally Described in Medical Literature
description: Michael Nahm and Bruce Greyson publish the first systematic scholarly review of terminal lucidity — unexpected return of clear consciousness in individuals with severe neurological conditions shortly before death — in the Journal of Nervous and Mental Disease, establishing the term and reviewing historical and contemporary cases.
sources: Nahm, M. and Greyson, B., Journal of Nervous and Mental Disease 197(12), 942–944 (2009)

title: Bem Publishes 'Feeling the Future' in JPSP
description: Social psychologist Daryl Bem publishes nine experiments reporting apparent retroactive influence on cognition in the Journal of Personality and Social Psychology. The publication prompts extensive scientific debate about replication standards, publication bias, and the evidential threshold for anomalous phenomena in psychology.
sources: Bem, D.J., Journal of Personality and Social Psychology 100(3), 407–425 (2011)

title: New York Times Discloses AATIP Programme
description: Journalists Leslie Kean, Ralph Blumenthal, and Helene Cooper publish an article in the New York Times disclosing the existence of the Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program (AATIP) and publishing the Nimitz FLIR1 video, which DoD subsequently confirms is authentic. The article is widely considered a turning point in the public and institutional engagement with UAP. Former AATIP director Luis Elizondo is named as a source.
sources: Kean, L., Blumenthal, R., and Cooper, H., New York Times, December 16, 2017
DoD confirmation statement, December 16, 2017

title: USS Omaha Sphere Footage
description: Spherical unidentified object filmed by naval personnel from the USS Omaha. The object reportedly descends into the ocean without impact debris. Footage corroborated by multiple crew members.
sources: DoD FOIA release, May 2021
The Debrief, May 14, 2021

title: DoD Officially Releases UAP Videos
description: The U.S. Department of Defense officially releases three infrared sensor videos — the Nimitz FLIR1 (2004), Gimbal, and GoFast (both 2015) — confirming their authenticity and acknowledging that the objects depicted 'remain unexplained.' This is the first official U.S. government release of UAP sensor footage.
sources: DoD press release, April 27, 2020
Official video release, media.defense.gov

title: UAPTF Preliminary Assessment Released
description: The U.S. Office of the Director of National Intelligence releases the UAP Task Force Preliminary Assessment, the first official public UAP analysis report since Project Blue Book. The report reviews 144 cases from government sources between 2004 and 2021; 143 are classified as unexplained. The document identifies five potential explanatory categories and calls for improved data collection standards.
sources: ODNI, Preliminary Assessment: Unidentified Aerial Phenomena, June 25, 2021

title: All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) Established
description: The Department of Defense establishes the All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) as the primary U.S. government body for UAP investigation, superseding the UAP Task Force. AARO's mandate covers aerial, sub-surface, and near-space anomalies. The FY2022 National Defense Authorization Act requires annual Congressional reporting and creates whistleblower protections for personnel with UAP-related information.
sources: DoD press release, July 20, 2022
National Defense Authorization Act FY2022, Section 1683

title: Congressional Hearing: UAP Whistleblower Testimony
description: The House Committee on Oversight and Accountability holds a public hearing on UAP at which Commander Ryan Graves (ret.) and Commander David Fravor (ret.) testify about their military UAP encounters. Former intelligence officer David Grusch testifies under oath that the U.S. government operates a multi-decade programme to recover and analyse non-human technology. AARO has not corroborated Grusch's claims in public reporting.
sources: House Committee on Oversight and Accountability hearing transcript, July 26, 2023
Graves, R., written testimony, July 2023
Grusch, D., written testimony, July 2023
